mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, which systematically reduces corporate profit growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and durable goods sectors, which amplifies earnings revision cycles and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns when only one series expands, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, triggering short-term momentum flows into cyclical equities and generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or the signal predictive power vanishes in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly supports wage income and aggregate consumption, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01 across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches the 200.0 threshold, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of periods following CPIAUCSL crossing 200.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for real rates.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with elevated federal funds rates creates a policy mismatch where monetary tightening outpaces economic momentum, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%, or the hypothesis is rejected when GDPC1 growth remains above 2.5% despite rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment confirms a synchronized economic contraction, which forces equity analysts to downgrade earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a walk-forward validation.
